exampleSentences: Khi nghe tiếng chuông báo cháy, mọi người lao ra ngoài rất nhanh.
Em nhỏ vô tình lao vào sân khi đang đuổi theo quả bóng.
Anh ấy lao đến giúp chị khi thấy chị bị ngã.

definitions.vi: Làm việc chăm chỉ hoặc lao động một cách mãnh liệt.
definitions.en: To work hard or labor intensively.

example.en: He works very hard and never seems to get tired.
example.vi: Ông ấy làm việc rất chăm chỉ, không biết mệt mỏi.
example.es: Él trabaja muy duro y nunca parece cansarse.

exampleSentencesTranslated: When they heard the fire alarm, everyone rush outside very quickly.
The child accidentally rush into the yard while chasing the ball.
He rush to help her when he saw her fall.
notes:
Lao = to rush, dash, or throw yourself quickly toward something. It often feels sudden and a bit forceful, sometimes almost out of control.

Used for fast physical movement: people, animals, vehicles, even objects. It’s stronger and more intense than chạy (just “run”).  

It can also mean “throw oneself into” an activity (like starting something with lots of energy). Close synonym: xông vào (rush in, often more aggressive). Opposite idea: dừng lại, chậm lại.

translation.en: I have to work hard on this.

content.sentence: Chúng ta cần phải ___ cho tương lai.

translation.en: We need to work hard for the future.

notes_md:
**Lao** = to rush, dash, or throw yourself quickly toward something. It often feels sudden and a bit forceful, sometimes almost out of control.

Used for fast physical movement: people, animals, vehicles, even objects. It’s stronger and more intense than *chạy* (just “run”).  


It can also mean “throw oneself into” an activity (like starting something with lots of energy). Close synonym: *xông vào* (rush in, often more aggressive). Opposite idea: *dừng lại*, *chậm lại*.
